title: Fix Obsidian broker fetch binding and rerun GUI smoke

do_not_read: other directories in ~/dev unless explicitly referenced

scope: Fix the Obsidian/Electron runtime transport failure where BrokerClient stores an unbound Window.fetch and then calls it as an instance method, causing `Illegal invocation` before any broker request reaches the mock broker.
goals: Use TDD: first add a protocol-client regression test that fails when the default global fetch requires the global/window receiver, then implement the smallest fix.
Bind or wrap the default global fetch so BrokerClient works in browser/Electron hosts while preserving custom fetchImpl injection for tests.
Regenerate clients/obsidian/main.js and keep the artifact freshness check passing.
Run npm test --prefix clients and relevant syntax/build checks.
Rerun the disposable-vault Obsidian GUI mock smoke; verify a broker request is sent, ghost text appears, Tab accepts it, and note.md changes by the mock insertion.
If the mock smoke passes and it is safe, run one bounded pi-backed Obsidian Markdown request with synthetic note content only.
non_goals: Do not change provider logic or broker protocol semantics.
Do not publish or install globally.
Do not send repository content to pi.
safety: Use disposable vaults and synthetic notes.
Use strict timeouts for real pi.
success_criteria: A regression test fails before the fix and passes after the fix for global fetch binding behavior.
node clients/obsidian/scripts/build-artifact.js --check and node --check clients/obsidian/main.js pass after regenerating the artifact.
Real Obsidian disposable-vault mock GUI smoke passes: no failed-load toast, broker POST observed, visible ghost text screenshot captured, Tab acceptance changes note.md.
If mock passes, bounded pi-backed Obsidian Markdown smoke is attempted or explicitly skipped with a safety reason.
